title: potentilla fastigiata var. permollis
intro: Potentilla fastigiata var. permollis, also known as the shrubby cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species in the rose family native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. The medicinal properties of Potentilla fastigiata var. permollis lie in its ability to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to protect against cell damage and infections. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used topically as a skin and wound treatment.
